heading: Targeting assistance to specific populations
label: 262g–1 Targeting assistance to specific populations
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Aug. 13, 1981section 1372 of Pub. L. 97–35section 290i of this title

children.heading: Congressional findings
children.label: a Congressional findings
children.text: The Congress finds that there is a need for concerted international efforts to deal with the problems of malnutrition, low life expectancy, childhood disease, underemployment, and low productivity in developing countries.

children.heading: Assistance to poorest populations
children.label: b Assistance to poorest populations
children.text: The Congress notes with approval that the Inter-American Development Bank, under the terms of its Fifth Replenishment, has adopted the target that 50 percent of its lending benefit the poorest groups and has developed a usable methodology for determining the proportion of its lending which benefits such groups.
